chapter_title.en: Reward of meeting a Hajj pilgrim and shaking hands with him

text: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُوسَی بْنِ الْمُتَوَکِّلِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ سَهْلِ بْنِ زِیَادٍ عَنْ یَعْقُوبَ بْنِ یَزِیدَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ حَمْزَهَ عَمَّنْ حَدَّثَهُ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ لَقِیَ حَاجّاً فَصَافَحَهُ کَانَ کَمَنِ اسْتَلَمَ الْحَجَرَ.

en.sayed-athar-husain-rizvi-&-sayed-maqs: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Musa b. al-Mutawakil who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Ja’far from Sahl b. Ziyad from Ya’qub b. Yazid from Muhammad b. Hamzah from the one the person that narrated to him from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “One who meets a Hajj pilgrim and shakes hands with him is as if he has kissed Hajar-e-Aswad.”
